quality.rationale: 利用电磁场标量积的洛伦兹不变性进行逻辑推理+狭义相对论基础应用

origin.question: Problem 4.3. A particular electromagnetic field has its E field at an angle $\theta_0$ to its B field, and $\theta_0$ is invariant to all observers. What is the value of $\theta_0$ ?
origin.solution:
In any frame the angle is given by
$$
\cos \theta_{0} = \underline {{{\mathbf {E}}}} \cdot \underline {{{\mathbf {B}}}} / | \mathbf {E} | | \mathbf {B} |.
$$
Now $\underline{\mathbf{E}}\cdot \underline{\mathbf{B}}$ is invariant (see Problem 4.2), but $|\mathbf{E}||\mathbf{B}|$ is not. Thus, $\theta_0$ can be invariant if and only if $\underline{\mathbf{E}}\cdot \underline{\mathbf{B}} = 0$ , i.e. $\theta_0 = \pi /2$ .
